mechanism_narrative: Sustained rise in real retail sales indicates strong consumer demand, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases but SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a threshold of 4.5%, it signals labor market weakening that reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative short-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 4.5% but SPX 5-day returns are positive more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and rise in the federal funds rate indicates tightening financial conditions that depress residential investment and broader economic activity, weighing on equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (declining HOUST + rising FEDFUNDS) is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-inflation regime defined by CPIAUCSL below 200, stable to falling Fed funds rate reinforces accommodative policy, supporting equity valuations and forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If in the low-inflation regime (CPIAUCSL < 200) the combination of low/falling FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 20-day SPX returns with hit rate > 55%, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Three consecutive monthly declines in real retail sales signal a contraction in consumer spending that precedes economic slowdown, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If a 3-month RSAFS decline is followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 30% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth combined with stable or declining Fed funds rate indicates a robust labor market and accommodative monetary policy, jointly boosting equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If during periods of rising PAYEMS and declining FEDFUNDS, SPX 20-day returns are negative more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below the index level of 110, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output that typically precedes broader economic weakness, leading to negative equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO < 110 is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present values and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If a falling FEDFUNDS does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns with a win rate above 52% over any 2-year rolling window, the hypothesis fails.
